report_name: China-Linked Group UNC6508 Bypasses Defenses for Over a Year in Massive Research Data Heist

threat_actors.name: UNC6508
threat_actors.description: A China-nexus threat actor that conducted a multi-year cyberespionage campaign against medical, academic, and military research organizations in the U.S. and Canada, active since at least 2023. The group exploited REDCap servers to exfiltrate sensitive data related to AI, defense, and medical research.
threat_actors.goals: Intelligence gathering on U.S. defense strategy, artificial intelligence, cyber warfare programs, and medical research, including the Chikungunya virus.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: UNC6508 exploited vulnerabilities in public-facing, legacy instances of REDCap servers to gain initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Windows Command Shell
attack_patterns.description: The InfiniteRed malware likely used the Windows Command Shell for command execution as part of its backdoor functionality.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping
attack_patterns.description: The InfiniteRed malware likely employed OS Credential Dumping techniques to harvest user credentials for lateral movement.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The InfiniteRed malware established command and control communications using application layer protocols to blend in with normal network traffic.

attack_patterns.name: Indicator Removal
attack_patterns.description: UNC6508 used various methods to evade detection, including removing indicators of their activity from compromised hosts.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: After establishing a foothold, UNC6508 pivoted to sensitive internal systems using remote services.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor staged and exfiltrated collected data over their command and control channel.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: UNC6508 likely used encoded PowerShell commands for fileless malware execution, often spawned from compromised web server processes.

malware.name: InfiniteRed
malware.description: A custom malware toolset deployed by UNC6508 after gaining initial access. It functions as a dropper, backdoor for persistent remote access, and a credential harvester.

vulnerabilities.name: REDCap Public-Facing Web Application Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: Unspecified vulnerabilities in unpatched, legacy versions of public-facing REDCap web servers were exploited by UNC6508 to gain initial access.

identities.name: Medical research organizations
identities.description: A class of victim organizations targeted by UNC6508 for sensitive medical research data, including world-renowned clinical providers.

identities.name: Academic research organizations
identities.description: A class of victim organizations, including premier academic centers, targeted by UNC6508 for research data.

identities.name: Military research organizations
identities.description: A class of victim organizations, including North American military health institutions, targeted by UNC6508 for data on defense strategy, AI, and unmanned vehicles.

identities.name: Health regulatory bodies
identities.description: A class of victim organizations targeted by UNC6508 in their cyberespionage campaign.

identities.name: REDCap
identities.description: The provider of the REDCap clinical research platform, whose legacy versions were exploited in the campaign.

infrastructure.name: UNC6508 C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Command and control infrastructure used by the InfiniteRed malware to exfiltrate data and receive commands. The actor used obfuscation networks and bulk-sourced accounts to hide this infrastructure.

indicators.name: Suspicious REDCap Web Requests
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious REDCap Web Requests
id: 59a1b2c3-d4e5-4f6a-b7c8-d9e0f1a2b3c4
status: experimental
description: Detects unusual web requests to REDCap application directories, which could indicate reconnaissance or exploitation attempts.
references:
  - https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/threat-intelligence/prc-targets-us-medical-research
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-15
tags:
  - attack.t1190
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains: '/redcap/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects unusual web requests to REDCap application directories, which could indicate reconnaissance or exploitation attempts as seen in the UNC6508 campaign.

indicators.name: Suspicious File Creation in REDCap Web Root
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious File Creation in REDCap Web Root
id: 69b1c2d3-e4f5-4a6b-b7c8-d9e0f1a2b3c5
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of potential webshells in the REDCap web root directory on IIS servers.
references:
  - https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/threat-intelligence/prc-targets-us-medical-research
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-15
tags:
  - attack.t1505.003
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|startswith: 'C:\inetpub\wwwroot\redcap\'
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.php'
      - '.asp'
      - '.aspx'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of potential webshells in the REDCap web root directory on IIS servers, a technique used by UNC6508 to deploy malware.

indicators.name: Suspicious Child Process of IIS Worker Process
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Child Process of IIS Worker Process
id: 79c1d2e3-f4a5-4b6c-b7c8-d9e0f1a2b3c6
status: experimental
description: Detects the IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) spawning command-line interpreters, a common pattern for webshell execution.
references:
  - https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/threat-intelligence/prc-targets-us-medical-research
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-15
tags:
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.t1059.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) spawning command-line interpreters, a common pattern for webshell execution as used by UNC6508.

indicators.name: Encoded PowerShell Command Execution from Web Server
indicators.pattern:
title: Encoded PowerShell Command Execution from Web Server
id: 89d1e2f3-a4b5-4c6d-b7c8-d9e0f1a2b3c7
status: experimental
description: Detects encoded PowerShell commands being executed by a web server process, a technique often used for fileless malware execution.
references:
  - https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/threat-intelligence/prc-targets-us-medical-research
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-15
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: ' -enc '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects encoded PowerShell commands being executed by a web server process, a technique often used for fileless malware execution and observed in UNC6508's TTPs.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious File Creation in REDCap Web Root
Suspicious Child Process of IIS Worker Process
Encoded PowerShell Command Execution from Web Server
